cross_scene_arcs.title: From Witness to Avenger: The Trauma-Fueled Path to Resistance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Asael's journey begins with helpless witness to his family's deportation and culminates in his active participation in violent retaliation. His initial goal of understanding and protecting his family collapses into grief, then transforms into a drive for vengeance as he fully embraces armed resistance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Desire to act against family's deportation, but constrained by survival and silence.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Full immersion in retribution through the killing of a German soldier, marking psychological transformation into an avenger.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral and psychological toll of participating in brutal violence remains unprocessed and may lead to internal conflict.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Guardianship and Emotional Burden: Asael's Role as Protector
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After discovering Aron alive, Asael assumes the role of caregiver and emotional anchor, which evolves into broader responsibility for the group's well-being. His relationship with Aron becomes the foundation for his commitment to collective survival and unity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Attempts to reconnect with traumatized Aron, taking on unexpected guardianship.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Publicly affirms group identity as 'Bielski Otriad,' demonstrating leadership rooted in care and inclusion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional toll of caregiving persists amid resource scarcity and ongoing trauma, threatening his resilience.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Panic to Tactical Resilience: Survival Under Fire
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Asael experiences a crisis of survival when he panics during an ambush and becomes isolated under enemy pursuit. Through quick thinking and brotherly intervention, he survives and returns, transformed from a liability into a re-integrated, trusted member.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Panic leads to flight on the wrong side of the road, isolating him and drawing enemy attention.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Successfully evades capture by hiding in an irrigation ditch and is later rescued, returning with supplies and new members.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether his earlier panic will affect future trust or leadership roles under pressure remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rising Responsibility: Asael's Ascension in the Camp Hierarchy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Asael evolves from a silent survivor to a key operational figure, taking on roles in vigilance, logistics, naming the group, and leading defense. His status rises from dependent to indispensable leader, entrusted with life-or-death missions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Support role during first raid, maintaining watch without direct action.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Entrusted with sacrificial rear-guard mission to delay enemy forces, symbolizing ultimate responsibility and leadership.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His survival in high-risk roles is uncertain, and the emotional cost of repeated sacrifice may challenge his continued effectiveness.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Crossroads: Commitment, Coercion, and the Cost of Compassion
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Asael is drawn into escalating moral dilemmas—promising to rescue Chaya's parents under emotional pressure, venturing into hostile territory, conducting covert arms operations, and ultimately accepting a suicide mission. His choices reflect a blend of compassion, duty, and entrapment in a cycle of sacrifice.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Emotionally overwhelmed by Chaya's plea, he makes a binding promise without assessing feasibility.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Commits to a potentially fatal delay mission, separating from Chaya and embracing martyrdom for the group's survival.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The consequences of his promise to save Chaya's parents remain unfulfilled, and his repeated self-sacrifice raises questions about agency versus obligation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Leadership: From Family Protector to Community Leader
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tuvia evolves from a brother returning to protect his immediate family into the moral and practical leader of a growing Jewish refuge, expanding his sense of responsibility from kinship to communal survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tuvia reestablishes emotional bonds with Asael and Aron, assuming responsibility for their safety after their parents' deaths.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tuvia leads a coordinated evacuation under fire, prioritizing the survival of all group members and entrusting critical roles to others.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The sustainability of Tuvia's leadership under relentless pressure and the long-term viability of the refuge remain uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Brotherhood: The Tuvia-Zus Conflict
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: A deep ideological and personal rift grows between Tuvia and his brother Zus, reflecting opposing philosophies of resistance—moral restraint versus violent retribution—culminating in a power struggle that threatens the unity of the otriad.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tension emerges between Tuvia and Zus upon Tuvia's return, foreshadowing future conflict over leadership and values.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Zus openly defies Tuvia and rejects reconciliation, while Viktor's arrival further isolates Tuvia, leaving his authority in jeopardy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The ideological divide between restraint and vengeance remains unresolved, and the future of the Bielski group's cohesion is uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Transformation: Vengeance, Mercy, and the Cost of Command
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tuvia's journey oscillates between acts of brutal vengeance and moments of profound mercy, revealing an internal struggle to reconcile justice with humanity, ultimately leading to a crisis of identity and a reluctant acceptance of symbolic leadership.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tuvia executes the Police Captain and his sons in a decisive act of retribution, embracing the role of executioner.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite rejecting leadership, Tuvia is sanctified by Shimon's dying words, elevating him to a near-messianic figure against his will.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tuvia's inner conflict between moral responsibility, personal guilt, and the weight of symbolic leadership remains unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Collapse and Renewal: The Psychological Toll of Leadership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tuvia's psychological state deteriorates under the weight of leadership, culminating in a breakdown and rejection of his role, only to be temporarily restored through crisis and the return of Asael, highlighting the fragility of his authority.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tuvia's drunken enforcement of rations undermines his credibility and strains his relationship with Asael.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: After a complete emotional collapse, Tuvia regains resolve through Asael's survival and Shimon's sacrifice, re-engaging in leadership despite inner turmoil.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tuvia's long-term psychological resilience and ability to sustain leadership remain in question despite temporary recovery.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Love, Loss, and Identity: Tuvia's Emotional Isolation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tuvia's emotional journey is marked by separation, grief, and tentative reconnection, as he grapples with the loss of his wife, his fractured family, and the possibility of new bonds with Lilka and Chaya.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tuvia leaves his wife Rifka to reunite with his family, sacrificing personal safety and marital stability for duty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tuvia shows vulnerability over his failed marriage and begins to form new emotional connections, particularly through his concern for Lilka and support of Asael's relationship with Chaya.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tuvia's unresolved guilt and grief suggest ongoing inner conflict about his capacity for love and connection.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Survival to Vengeance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zus evolves from seeking basic survival and aid to embracing a violent ideology of retribution, shifting his primary goal from passive endurance to active vengeance against anti-Semitic Poles.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Secure food and aid from the farmer to sustain survival.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The local population is now aware of the Bielski Otriad's presence and mission, but the operation risks drawing dangerous attention, creating tension with Tuvia's strategy of caution.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The growing ideological and strategic divergence between Zus and Tuvia threatens unity within the group.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fracturing Brotherhood
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zus's relationship with Tuvia deteriorates from reluctant deference to open rivalry, culminating in a complete ideological and emotional break as he rejects Tuvia's leadership and the family bond.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Zus stepping back from the role of caretaker, recognizing Tuvia as the new emotional and symbolic leader.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Zus remains emotionally detached and physically withdrawn, signaling his complete separation from Tuvia and the group's cause.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The ideological and fraternal rift between Zus and Tuvia remains unhealed, with no indication of future reconciliation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Descent and Leadership Ascent
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zus ascends in status within the group through acts of violence and decisive action, transforming from a reactive survivor into a dominant, feared leader who commands through force and weapon distribution.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Zus successfully positions himself as a co-leader by distributing spoils and issuing orders, gaining symbolic power through the act of gifting Tuvia a weapon as a trophy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The wire is successfully anchored to a tree, establishing a key component of the ambush setup.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The tension between Zus's growing assertiveness and Tuvia's authority may lead to future power struggles within the group's leadership.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Protector of the Vulnerable to Advocate of the Ruthless
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zus grapples with an internal conflict between his capacity for compassion—seen in his care for Aron and Asael—and his belief in ruthless pragmatism, ultimately favoring a survivalist ideology that sacrifices the vulnerable for strength.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: assumption of guardianship and expanded moral responsibility toward a vulnerable survivor
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The enemy is psychologically destabilized, leading to their retreat; however, internal tension emerges as Zus criticizes Tuvia's leadership and leniency.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The conflict between Zus's belief in lethal deterrence and Tuvia's policy of restraint remains unresolved, foreshadowing deeper ideological divisions within the group.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Loyalty Tested by Ideology
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Zus confronts systemic antisemitism within the Russian partisans and the expendability of his family, leading to a crisis of loyalty where his commitment to ideology collapses in favor of blood ties—though too late to reconcile with his brothers.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gramov issues a hollow, degrading apology ('I apologize, Jew'), and Panchenko dismisses the incident as resolved, revealing the limits of institutional support for Jewish partisans.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Zus's trust in the Russian partisans is shattered, marking the beginning of his ideological disillusionment and emotional detachment from their cause.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Zus is left torn between his duty to the partisan movement and his loyalty to his family, with no clear path forward.
